points.text: Ona Therapeutics raised $86.6 million in Series B funding to advance a pair of antibody-drug conjugates (ADCs) for breast and colorectal cancers.
Abzena reports increased demand for formulation services supporting next-generation bioconjugate drugs and novel cargo conjugation to antibodies.

Shattuck Labs, Inc. — Clinical-stage biotechnology company — Monoclonal and bispecific DR3 blocking antibodies — Announced pricing of $75 million public offering to fund development of first-in-class monoclonal antibodies for inflammatory and immune-mediated diseases.
Windward Bio advanced WIN378, a fully human monoclonal antibody targeting TSLP, into Phase 2 SIRIUS study for COPD with ultra long-acting twice-yearly dosing potential.
Windward Bio secured $165M in crossover financing led by OrbiMed to advance its pipeline of long-acting immunology therapies including monoclonal antibody WIN378.
Kyowa Kirin discontinued all clinical trials for rocatinlimab, an anti-OX40 monoclonal antibody, following safety reviews identifying emerging concerns of malignancies including Kaposi's sarcoma.
FDA updated the Tremfya (guselkumab) label to include inhibition of structural joint damage progression in adults with active psoriatic arthritis based on APEX trial data.
Johnson & Johnson — Nipocalimab — Systemic Lupus Erythematosus — Phase 2 study demonstrated significant reduction in disease activity; drug is an FcRn blocker monoclonal antibody.

University of Montreal / Dr. André Veillette — Anti-SLAMF6 antibodies — Cancer immunotherapy — Developed monoclonal antibodies blocking SLAMF6 to prevent T-cell exhaustion; showed strong anti-tumor responses in mice.
Connect Biopharma is advancing rademikibart, a humanized monoclonal antibody targeting IL-4Rα, for acute asthma and COPD exacerbations via a dual inhibition strategy.
Sanofi's subcutaneous isatuximab-irfc (Sarclisa), a monoclonal antibody, received EU approval for multiple myeloma across all existing indications for the intravenous formulation.

Mapp Biopharmaceuticals — MBP-134 — Ebola — HHS confirmed access to this experimental antibody treatment for Americans with high-risk exposures; product developed with BARDA funding but efficacy in humans not yet established via clinical trial.

Bio X Cell — Anti-mouse PD-L1 × TIGIT × LAG3 trispecific antibody — Preclinical research — Launched first commercially available ready-to-use trispecific format to study combinatorial checkpoint biology in vivo, expanding beyond traditional monoclonal antibody tools.
Ona Therapeutics — ADC pipeline — Breast and Colorectal Cancer — Secured $86.6M Series B to launch two antibody-drug conjugates into clinical development.

Abzena — Formulation Services — Biologics and Bioconjugates — Deploying 'Fast-to-Clinic' and 'Best-in-Clinic' approaches to address instability in bi/multi-specific formats and antibody-cargo conjugates.

Shattuck Labs — Monoclonal and bispecific DR3 blocking antibodies — Inflammatory and immune-mediated diseases — Completed pricing of public offering for 10,879,376 shares at $4.00/share and pre-funded warrants for 7,870,624 shares at $3.9999/warrant, raising ~$75M gross proceeds.

Windward Bio — WIN378 (anti-TSLP monoclonal antibody) — Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease (COPD) — Dosed first patients in Phase 2 SIRIUS study; ultra long-acting formulation planned for twice-yearly dosing.
Windward Bio — WIN378 — Asthma — Phase 2/3 POLARIS program dose-ranging component fully recruited; initial data expected H2 2026; Phase 3 study start expected Q4 2026.

Kyowa Kirin — Rocatinlimab — Atopic Dermatitis, Asthma, Prurigo Nodularis — Discontinued all Phase 2 and 3 trials after safety review identified one confirmed and one suspected case of Kaposi's sarcoma linked to OX40 pathway modulation.

Janssen — Tremfya (guselkumab) — Psoriatic Arthritis — FDA label expansion approved to include inhibition of structural joint damage progression; APEX trial showed significantly lower radiographic progression vs placebo at 24 weeks in >1,000 biologic-naïve adults.

University of Montreal — Anti-SLAMF6 monoclonal antibodies — Cancer — Preclinical development of antibodies blocking SLAMF6 self-binding to restore T-cell function; superior to existing SLAMF6 targeting approaches in mouse models.

Sanofi — Isatuximab-irfc (Sarclisa) — Multiple Myeloma — EU approval granted for subcutaneous on-body injector formulation based on Phase 3 IRAKLIA trial showing noninferiority to IV (ORR 71.7% vs 70.5%; P=.0006).
Connect Biopharma — Rademikibart — Acute Asthma/COPD Exacerbations — Humanized monoclonal antibody targeting IL-4Rα advancing in global clinical studies for acute exacerbations.

Mapp Biopharmaceuticals — MBP-134 — Ebola — HHS authorized access for high-risk US exposures; antibody developed with BARDA funding; dose inventory undisclosed as BARDA owns stock; no human clinical trial data available.

Bio X Cell — Anti-mouse PD-L1 × TIGIT × LAG3 — Preclinical oncology research — Released first ready-to-use trispecific antibody (murine IgG2a constant region) enabling simultaneous interrogation of three checkpoint pathways in standard mouse models.
Ona Therapeutics — ADC commercialization — Breast and Colorectal Cancer pipeline — $86.6M Series B raised June 2026 to initiate clinical trials.
Abzena — Formulation development for bioconjugates — Antibody conjugation services — Addressing instability in bi/multi-specific formats and novel cargo conjugation.

Shattuck Labs — Pioneering first-in-class monoclonal and bispecific DR3 blocking antibodies — Pipeline for inflammatory and immune-mediated diseases — Secured $75M capital raise via public offering to advance clinical-stage monoclonal antibody programs.

Windward Bio — Commercialization of long-acting monoclonal antibodies — WIN378 (anti-TSLP mAb) and WIN027 (bispecific TSLP/IL-13) — $165M crossover financing secured; total raised $365M since Jan 2025.

Kyowa Kirin — Monoclonal antibody safety management — Rocatinlimab (anti-OX40 mAb) — Halted global development program due to malignancy signals; previously reported durable efficacy in ROCKET program for atopic dermatitis.

Janssen — Label expansion for structural damage inhibition — Tremfya (guselkumab) — Fully human monoclonal antibody targeting IL-23p19; now indicated for inhibition of structural joint damage in active psoriatic arthritis.
Johnson & Johnson — FcRn blockade commercialization — Nipocalimab (monoclonal antibody) — Phase 2 data supports advancement in systemic lupus erythematosus indication.

University of Montreal / Montreal Clinical Research Institute — Novel cancer immunotherapy platform — Anti-SLAMF6 monoclonal antibodies — Preclinical stage; targeting patients resistant to PD1/PDL1 inhibitors.

Sanofi — Commercialization of subcutaneous monoclonal antibody delivery — Isatuximab-irfc (Sarclisa) — EU approval secured for on-body injector; Phase 3 data supports noninferior efficacy and reduced infusion reactions (1.5% vs 25%).
Connect Biopharma — Development of dual-cytokine targeting biologic — Rademikibart (anti-IL-4Rα mAb) — Clinical-stage program addressing unmet need in acute respiratory exacerbations.

Mapp Biopharmaceuticals — Emergency Ebola countermeasure development — MBP-134 (antibody) — Product authorized for US high-risk exposure access; manufacturing and inventory controlled by BARDA; human efficacy data pending.

Bio X Cell — Expansion into multispecific antibody formats — Anti-mouse PD-L1 × TIGIT × LAG3 — Transitioning beyond traditional monoclonal antibodies to offer recombinant trispecific tools for in vivo checkpoint biology research.

Ona Therapeutics ADCs — Mechanism: Antibody-drug conjugate linking protein to toxic payload — Phase: Pre-clinical to Clinical initiation — Population: Advanced breast and colorectal cancer — Status: Funding secured for development.

Antibody-cargo conjugates — Mechanism: Conjugation of novel cargos to antibodies — Challenge: Inherent instability requiring considered formulation approaches — Context: Increasing demand for high-concentration forms in next-generation biologics.

Shattuck Labs monoclonal and bispecific DR3 blocking antibodies — Mechanism: DR3 receptor blockade — Phase: Clinical-stage — Population: Patients with inflammatory and immune-mediated diseases — Status: First-in-class candidates advancing with $75M funding secured.

WIN378 — Mechanism: Fully human monoclonal antibody binding thymic stromal lymphopoietin (TSLP) ligand — Phase: 2 (SIRIUS) and 2/3 (POLARIS) — Population: COPD and Asthma patients — Dosing: Ultra long-acting, twice-yearly — Status: First patient dosed in COPD study Q2 2026.

Rocatinlimab — Mechanism: Anti-OX40 monoclonal antibody targeting memory T cells — Phase: 2/3 (discontinued) — Population: Moderate to severe atopic dermatitis, uncontrolled asthma, prurigo nodularis — Comparator: N/A (trial halted) — Results: Development halted due to Kaposi's sarcoma cases suggesting mechanistic link to OX40 modulation.

Guselkumab — Mechanism: Fully human monoclonal antibody binding CD64 and inhibiting IL-23p19 subunit — Phase: 3 (APEX) — Population: >1,000 biologic-naïve adults with active psoriatic arthritis — Comparator: Placebo — Endpoint: Radiographic progression at 24 weeks; results showed significantly lower progression rates.

Nipocalimab — Mechanism: FcRn blockade reducing pathogenic IgG — Phase: 2 — Population: Systemic Lupus Erythematosus — Comparator: Not specified in source — Results: Significant reduction in disease activity reported.

Anti-SLAMF6 antibodies — Mechanism: Monoclonal blockade of SLAMF6 self-association to prevent T-cell exhaustion — Phase: Preclinical — Population: Murine tumor models — Comparator: Existing PD1/PDL1 approaches — Results: Increased T-cell activation, reduced exhaustion, and strong anti-tumor responses observed.

Isatuximab-irfc (SC) — Mechanism: Anti-CD38 monoclonal antibody — Phase: 3 (IRAKLIA) — Population: Relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma — Comparator: Intravenous isatuximab — Endpoints/Results: ORR 71.7% vs 70.5% (noninferior); systemic infusion reactions 1.5% vs 25%; patient satisfaction 70% vs 53.4%.

Rademikibart — Mechanism: Humanized monoclonal antibody targeting IL-4Rα with dual inhibition strategy — Phase: Clinical studies — Population: Acute asthma and COPD exacerbations — Comparator: Not specified in source — Endpoints/Results: Designed for faster intervention than current options; specific trial data not provided.

MBP-134 — Mechanism: Antibody treatment (specific MOA not detailed in source) — Phase: Pre-clinical/Animal testing only — Population: Humans (high-risk Ebola exposure) — Comparator: None — Status: Efficacy in humans not yet established; authorized for emergency access.

Anti-mouse PD-L1 × TIGIT × LAG3 — Mechanism: Trispecific antibody targeting PD-L1, TIGIT, and LAG3 immune checkpoints — Phase: Preclinical tool — Population: Standard mouse models — Comparator: Multi-agent study designs — Utility: Reduces variability in studying T-cell exhaustion and immune suppression.
Ona Therapeutics — Series B financing — ADC development — Raised $86.6M to fund clinical launch of differentiated antibody-drug conjugates.

Shattuck Labs — Public offering — $75 million gross proceeds — Issued 10,879,376 common shares at $4.00 and 7,870,624 pre-funded warrants at $3.9999; underwriters granted 30-day option for 2,812,500 additional shares; joint bookrunners: Leerink Partners, J.P. Morgan, Piper Sandler, Cantor.

Windward Bio — $165M crossover financing — Led by OrbiMed with participation from RA Capital, Janus Henderson, Sanofi Ventures, Harbour BioMed — Proceeds to advance WIN378 to Phase 3 and WIN027 to respiratory/dermatology studies by Q4 2026.
Sanofi — Regulatory filing and approval — Isatuximab-irfc (Sarclisa) — EU Committee for Medicinal Products for Human View issued positive opinion leading to approval of subcutaneous formulation.
Mapp Biopharmaceuticals — BARDA funding partnership — MBP-134 — BARDA owns all existing doses; inventory quantity undisclosed; no commercial sales reported.

Bio X Cell — Product launch — Anti-mouse PD-L1 × TIGIT × LAG3 — First commercially available ready-to-use trispecific antibody; manufactured to in vivo-ready quality standards with ultra-pure, carrier-free formulations.
